---
title: "GLM_Graphs_V2"
author: "Benhao Gu"
date: "2025-08-03"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
rm(list = ls())
library(tidyverse)
library(sjPlot)
library(lme4)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
```

```{r}
#Variable settings
#In both bio and pyro 
b_list = seq(0.02, 10, length.out = 100)
k_list = seq(0.5, 2, length.out = 100)
t = seq(0.001, 1000.001, length.out = 100001)

setwd("C:/Users/guben/OneDrive - The University of Melbourne/Unimelb/2025/Research/Simulation/R_data")
data <- read_csv("species_power_dist.csv")
spec(data)

species_list = data$species
alpha_list = data$alpha
beta_list = data$beta + 1
n = length(data$species)
sum(beta_list) #0.555
```

```{r}
# t = seq(0.001, 15.001, length.out = 16)

power_fun = function(t, alpha, beta){
  return(sapply(seq_along(alpha), function(i) alpha[i] * t^beta[i]))
}

gfunction = function(t_list, b, k){
  return(b^(1/k) * exp(-b * t_list^k) / gamma(1/k + 1))
}

# biodiversity index for fixed b, k and species
bio_index_thm = function(b, k){
  # This is the mathematically correct formula for the integral
  d = alpha_list * b^(-(beta_list - 1)/k) * gamma(beta_list / k) / gamma(1/k)  
  # The rest of the function remains the same
  power = sum(log(d))/n
  G = exp(power)
  return (log(G))
}

bio_index_num = function(b, k){
  # d = colSums(power_fun(t, alpha_list, beta_list) * gfunction(t, b, k)) * 1000/100001
  d = t(as.matrix(power_fun(t, alpha_list, beta_list))) %*% as.matrix(gfunction(t, b, k)) * 1000/100001
  power = sum(log(d))/n
  G = exp(power)
  return (log(G))
}

d_generator = function(b, k, alpha, beta){
  alpha_value = alpha
  beta_value = beta
  
  # functions
  power_fun_t = function(t){
    return (power_fun(t, alpha_value, beta_value))  
  }
  
  gfunction_t = function(t){
    return (gfunction(t, b, k))
  }
  
  bio_t = function(t){
    return (power_fun_t(t) * gfunction_t(t))
  }
  
  d = integrate(bio_t, 0, Inf)
  
  return (d)
  # power = sum(log(d))/n
  # G = exp(power)
  # return (log(G))
}

bio_index_auto = function(b, k){
  d = unlist(mapply(function(alpha, beta) {
  d_generator(b = b, k = k, alpha = alpha, beta = beta)$value
}, alpha_list, beta_list))
  
  power = sum(log(d))/n
  G = exp(power)
  return (log(G))
}
# 
# bio_index_auto(2, 0.5)
# 
# bio_index_auto(1.5, 0.5, alpha_list[1], beta_list[1])
# t = seq(0.001, 1000.001, length.out = 1000001)
# b = 0.5
# k = 1.5
# alpha_list * b^(-beta_list/k) * gamma((beta_list + 1)/k) / gamma(1/k)
# 
# 
# colSums(power_fun(t, alpha_list, beta_list) * gfunction(t, 1.5, 0.5)) * 1000/10001



# [1] -0.9455497
# [1] -0.9499783
system.time(bio_index_auto(2, 0.5))
system.time({bio_index_thm(2, 0.5)})
system.time(bio_index_num(2, 0.5))


bio_index_auto(2, 0.5)
bio_index_thm(2, 0.5)
bio_index_num(2, 0.5)
```


```{r}
b = 1.5
k = 0.5
t = c(1, 2)
  power_fun_t = function(t){
    return (power_fun(t, alpha_list[1], beta_list[1]))  
    }
  gfunction_t = function(t){
    return (gfunction(t, b, k))
  }
  bio_x = function(x){
    return (power_fun_t(x) * gfunction_t(x))
  }
  
  bio_x(1)
  
  d = integrate(bio_x, 0, Inf)

d

```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)

# --- Pyro index (unchanged formula) ---
pyro_index <- function(b, k) {
  1/k * (1 - log(b)) + lgamma(1/k + 1)
}

# --- Biodiversity index: using your pure GLM approach ---
# (Assuming bio_index_thm is defined in your environment)
bio_index <- function(b, k) {
  bio_index_thm(b, k)  # returns log(G) directly
}

# --- 1. Sweep b for fixed k values ---
b_vals <- seq(0.2, 3, length.out = 100)
# k_fixed <- c(0.6, 0.7, 1, 1.5, 2)
k_fixed <- c(1, 1.5, 2)

df1 <- expand.grid(b = b_vals, k = k_fixed)
df1$pyro <- mapply(pyro_index, df1$b, df1$k)
df1$logBio <- mapply(bio_index, df1$b, df1$k)

p1 <- ggplot(df1, aes(x = pyro, y = logBio, color = factor(k))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  
  # UPDATED THIS LINE to set both y-axis and x-axis limits
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1.4, -0.9), xlim = c(-0.7, 4.5)) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Pyro Index",
    y = "log(Geometric Species Index)",
    color = "k (fixed)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p1)

# --- 2. Sweep k for fixed b values ---
k_vals <- seq(5, 5.7, length.out = 50)
b_fixed <- c(2)

df2 <- expand.grid(b = b_fixed, k = k_vals)
df2$pyro <- mapply(pyro_index, df2$b, df2$k)
df2$logBio <- mapply(bio_index, df2$b, df2$k)

p2 <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = pyro, y = logBio, color = factor(b))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  
  # UPDATED THIS LINE to set both y-axis and x-axis limits
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1.4, -0.9), xlim = c(-0.7, 4.5)) +
  labs(
    x = "Pyro Index",
    y = "log(Geometric Species Index)",
    color = "b (fixed)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p2)
```

```{r}
# Load the ggplot2 library
library(ggplot2)

# --- Data Generation (assuming your functions are loaded) ---
# This part of your script remains the same
k_vals <- seq(0.4, 100, length.out = 50000)
# k_vals <- seq(1, 10, length.out = 50000) 
b_fixed <- c(0.4, 1.5, 3, 5)

df2 <- expand.grid(b = b_fixed, k = k_vals)
df2$pyro <- mapply(pyro_index, df2$b, df2$k)
df2$logBio <- mapply(bio_index, df2$b, df2$k)

# --- PLOTTING CODE WITH Y-AXIS LIMITS ---
p2_simple_trajectory <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = pyro, y = logBio, color = factor(b), group = b)) +
  geom_path(linewidth = 1.2) + 
  
  # ADDED THIS LINE to set the y-axis limits
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1.4, -0.8), xlim = c(-0.7, 4.5)) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Pyro Index",
    y = "log(Geometric Species Index)",
    color = "b (fixed)" # This creates the legend title
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p2_simple_trajectory)
```
```{r}
# Load the ggplot2 library
library(ggplot2)

# --- Data Generation (assuming your functions are loaded) ---
# This part of your script remains the same
# k_vals <- seq(0.4, 100, length.out = 50000) 
k_vals <- seq(1, 2, length.out = 50000) 
b_fixed <- c(0.4, 1.5, 3, 5)

df2 <- expand.grid(b = b_fixed, k = k_vals)
df2$pyro <- mapply(pyro_index, df2$b, df2$k)
df2$logBio <- mapply(bio_index, df2$b, df2$k)

# --- PLOTTING CODE WITH Y-AXIS LIMITS ---
p2_simple_trajectory <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = pyro, y = logBio, color = factor(b), group = b)) +
  geom_path(linewidth = 1.2) + 
  
  # ADDED THIS LINE to set the y-axis limits
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1.4, -0.8), xlim = c(-0.7, 4.5)) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Pyro Index",
    y = "log(Geometric Species Index)",
    color = "b (fixed)" # This creates the legend title
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p2_simple_trajectory)
```

```{r}
# Load the ggplot2 library
library(ggplot2)

# --- Data Generation (same as your script) ---
k_vals <- seq(0.1, 10000, length.out = 50000) 
b_fixed <- c(2, 10)

df2 <- expand.grid(b = b_fixed, k = k_vals)
df2$pyro <- mapply(pyro_index, df2$b, df2$k)
df2$logBio <- mapply(bio_index, df2$b, df2$k)

# --- CORRECTED PLOTTING CODE ---
p2_simple_trajectory <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = pyro, y = logBio, color = factor(b), group = b)) +
  # Use geom_path to draw a line for each 'b' value
  geom_path(size = 1.2) + 
  
  labs(
    x = "Pyro Index",
    y = "log(Geometric Species Index)",
    color = "b (fixed)" # This creates the legend title
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p2_simple_trajectory)
```



```{r}
plot(df2$k, df2$pyro)
plot(df2$k, df2$logBio)

```


```{r}
# Load the ggplot2 and viridis libraries (for a better color scale)
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)

# --- 2. Sweep k for fixed b values ---
# It's good to use more points for a smoother line
# Horitontal Intercept
# k_vals <- seq(6, 10000, length.out = 50000)
# b_fixed <- c(0.9)

k_vals <- seq(0.1, 10000, length.out = 50000)
b_fixed <- c(50)

# k_vals <- seq(0.9, 10, length.out = 50000)
# b_fixed <- c(3)

# k_vals <- seq(0.9, 15, length.out = 50000)
# b_fixed <- c(4)

df2 <- expand.grid(b = b_fixed, k = k_vals)
df2$pyro <- mapply(pyro_index, df2$b, df2$k)
df2$logBio <- mapply(bio_index, df2$b, df2$k)

# --- PLOTTING CODE FOR A COLORED TRAJECTORY ---
p2_trajectory <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = pyro, y = logBio)) +
  # Use geom_path and map the color to the continuous 'k' value
  geom_path(aes(color = k),
            size = 1.2,
            # This adds an arrow to the end of the line to show direction
            arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.3, "cm"))) +

  # Use a color scale that is easy to read
  scale_color_viridis_c() +

  labs(
    x = "Pyro Index",
    y = "log(Geometric Species Index)",
    title = "Trajectory Plot for b = 1",
    color = "k" # Update the legend title
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p2_trajectory)


```

```{r}
pyro_index(3, 1.571)
bio_index(3, 1.571)

```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Clean df1
df1_clean <- df1 %>%
  filter(is.finite(pyro), is.finite(logBio)) %>%   # remove NaN/Inf
  arrange(k, pyro) %>%                             # order so lines plot sensibly
  group_by(k)

p1 <- ggplot(df1_clean, aes(x = pyro, y = logBio, color = factor(k), group = factor(k))) +
  geom_line(size = 1, na.rm = TRUE) +
  labs(x = "Pyro Index", y = "log(Geometric Species Index)", color = "k (fixed)") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p1)

# Clean df2
df2_clean <- df2 %>%
  filter(is.finite(pyro), is.finite(logBio)) %>%
  arrange(b, pyro) %>%
  group_by(b)

p2 <- ggplot(df2_clean, aes(x = pyro, y = logBio, color = factor(b), group = factor(b))) +
  geom_line(size = 1, na.rm = TRUE) +
  labs(x = "Pyro Index", y = "log(Geometric Species Index)", color = "b (fixed)") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p2)



```


```{r}
plot(df2$k ,df2$pyro)

plot(df2$k ,df2$logBio)

```
```{r}
# # Load necessary libraries for plotting and animation
# # Make sure you have these installed: install.packages(c("ggplot2", "gganimate", "gifski"))
# # install.packages("gifski")
# library(ggplot2)
# library(gganimate)
# library(gifski) # Used for rendering the animation as a GIF
# 
# # --- User's Original Code (for context) ---
# 
# # --- Pyro index (unchanged formula) ---
# pyro_index <- function(b, k) {
#   1/k * (1 - log(b)) + lgamma(1/k + 1)
# }
# 
# # --- Biodiversity index: using your pure GLM approach ---
# #     This assumes the existence of a function bio_index_auto(b, k)
# #     For this example to run, we'll create a placeholder for it based on the thesis formulas.
# #     You should replace this with your actual bio_index_auto function.
# A_vals <- c(1.3252,0.9700,0.4485,0.0806,0.2108,3.5818,1.8363,0.0096,0.8212,0.0297,0.3014)
# B_vals <- c(0.9019,0.5291,1.1164,1.3852,0.9919,0.7674,0.6559,1.7940,1.1084,1.5550,0.7506)
# n_species <- 11
# 
# bio_index_auto <- function(b, k) {
#   log_di <- log(A_vals) - ((B_vals - 1) / k) * log(b) + (lgamma(B_vals / k) - lgamma(1 / k))
#   log_G <- sum(log_di) / n_species
#   return(log_G)
# }
# 
# bio_index <- function(b, k) {
#   bio_index_auto(b, k)  # returns log(G) directly
# }
# 
# # --- Data generation for the case where k is varied ---
# k_vals <- seq(5, 5.7, length.out = 200) # Increased points for a smoother animation
# b_fixed <- c(2)
# 
# df2 <- expand.grid(b = b_fixed, k = k_vals)
# df2$pyro <- mapply(pyro_index, df2$b, df2$k)
# df2$logBio <- mapply(bio_index, df2$b, df2$k)
# 
# 
# # --- NEW ANIMATION CODE ---
# 
# #' Create an animated trajectory of the parametric curve.
# #'
# #' @param data A dataframe containing columns 'pyro', 'logBio', and 'k'.
# #' @param orientation A string, either "P_vs_lnG" (jagged plot) or "lnG_vs_P" (smooth plot).
# #' @return A gganim object.
# animate_trajectory <- function(data, orientation = "P_vs_lnG") {
# 
#   # Start with a base ggplot object
#   p <- ggplot(data)
# 
#   # Set the aesthetics based on the desired orientation
#   if (orientation == "P_vs_lnG") {
#     p <- p + aes(x = pyro, y = logBio) +
#       labs(
#         title = 'Tracing the curve for k = {round(frame_along, 2)}',
#         x = "Pyro Index",
#         y = "log(Geometric Species Index)"
#       )
#   } else {
#     p <- p + aes(x = logBio, y = pyro) +
#       labs(
#         title = 'Tracing the curve for k = {round(frame_along, 2)}',
#         x = "log(Geometric Species Index)",
#         y = "Pyro Index"
#       )
#   }
# 
#   # Add layers for the path and the animation
#   # geom_path traces the full trajectory in a light color
#   # geom_point shows the current position as a moving red dot
#   anim <- p +
#     geom_path(color = 'grey', size = 0.5) +
#     geom_point(color = 'red', size = 3) +
#     theme_minimal() +
#     # This is the core gganimate function: it reveals the data along the 'k' variable
#     transition_reveal(along = k) +
#     ease_aes('linear') # Ensures constant speed of animation
# 
#   return(anim)
# }
# 
# # --- Generate the two animations ---
# 
# # 1. Animation for the jagged plot (P on x-axis, ln(G) on y-axis)
# jagged_animation <- animate_trajectory(df2, orientation = "P_vs_lnG")
# 
# # 2. Animation for the smooth plot (ln(G) on x-axis, P on y-axis)
# smooth_animation <- animate_trajectory(df2, orientation = "lnG_vs_P")
# 
# 
# # --- Render and Save the Animations ---
# 
# # This will render the animation. In RStudio, it will appear in the Viewer pane.
# # It might take a moment to process.
# print(jagged_animation)
# print(smooth_animation)
# 
# jagged_animation_def <- animate_trajectory(df2, orientation = "P_vs_lnG")
# smooth_animation_def <- animate_trajectory(df2, orientation = "lnG_vs_P")
# 
# # Use anim_save() to save the animations to your working directory as GIF files.
# # anim_save() defaults to saving the last animation that was rendered.
# cat("Rendering jagged animation... This may take a moment.\n")
# jagged_rendered <- animate(jagged_animation_def, renderer = gifski_renderer())
# 
# cat("Rendering smooth animation... This may take a moment.\n")
# smooth_rendered <- animate(smooth_animation_def, renderer = gifski_renderer())
# 
# # Now, we can reliably save these rendered objects to files.
# cat("Saving animations to disk...\n")
# 
# anim_save("jagged_trajectory.gif", animation = jagged_rendered)
# anim_save("smooth_trajectory.gif", animation = smooth_rendered)
# 
# cat("Animations saved successfully to your working directory.\n")
# ```
# 
# 
# 
# ```{r}
# # --- Pyro index (unchanged formula) ---
# pyro_index <- function(b, k) {
#   1/k * (1 - log(b)) + lgamma(1/k + 1)
# }
# 
# # --- Biodiversity index: using your pure GLM approach ---
# #   You can swap bio_index_auto with bio_index_thm or bio_index_num
# bio_index <- function(b, k) {
#   bio_index_thm(b, k)  # returns log(G) directly
# }
# 
# # --- 1. Sweep b for fixed k values ---
# b_vals <- seq(0.2, 4, length.out = 100)
# k_fixed <- c(0.6, 0.7, 1, 1.5, 2)
# 
# df1 <- expand.grid(b = b_vals, k = k_fixed)
# df1$pyro <- mapply(pyro_index, df1$b, df1$k)
# df1$logBio <- mapply(bio_index, df1$b, df1$k)
# 
# p1 <- ggplot(df1, aes(x = pyro, y = logBio, color = factor(k))) +
#   geom_line(size = 1) +
#   labs(
#     x = "Pyro Index",
#     y = "log(Geometric Species Index)",
#     color = "k (fixed)"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal()
# 
# print(p1)
# 
# # --- 2. Sweep k for fixed b values ---
# k_vals <- seq(5, 5.7, length.out = 50)
# # b_fixed <- c(0.2, 0.5, 1, 2, 3)
# b_fixed <- c(2)
# 
# 
# df2 <- expand.grid(b = b_fixed, k = k_vals)
# df2$pyro <- mapply(pyro_index, df2$b, df2$k)
# df2$logBio <- mapply(bio_index, df2$b, df2$k)
# 
# p2 <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = logBio, y = pyro, color = factor(b))) +
#   geom_line(size = 1) +
#   labs(
#     x = "log(Geometric Species Index)",
#     y = "Pyro Index",
#     color = "b (fixed)"
#   ) +
#   theme_minimal()
# 
# print(p2)

```






```{r}
# --- Pyro index (unchanged formula) ---
pyro_index <- function(b, k) {
  1/k * (1 - log(b)) + lgamma(1/k + 1)
}

# --- Biodiversity index: using your pure GLM approach ---
#   You can swap bio_index_auto with bio_index_thm or bio_index_num
bio_index <- function(b, k) {
  bio_index_thm(b, k)  # returns log(G) directly
}

# --- 1. Sweep b for fixed k values ---
b_vals <- seq(0.2, 4, length.out = 100)
k_fixed <- c(0.6, 0.7, 1, 1.5, 2)

df1 <- expand.grid(b = b_vals, k = k_fixed)
df1$pyro <- mapply(pyro_index, df1$b, df1$k)
df1$logBio <- mapply(bio_index, df1$b, df1$k)

p1 <- ggplot(df1, aes(x = pyro, y = logBio, color = factor(k))) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Pyro Index",
    y = "log(Geometric Species Index)",
    color = "k (fixed)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p1)

# --- 2. Sweep k for fixed b values ---
k_vals <- seq(0.4, 10, length.out = 500)
# b_fixed <- c(0.2, 0.5, 1, 2, 3)
b_fixed <- c(3)


df2 <- expand.grid(b = b_fixed, k = k_vals)
df2$pyro <- mapply(pyro_index, df2$b, df2$k)
df2$logBio <- mapply(bio_index, df2$b, df2$k)

p2 <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = pyro, y = logBio, color = factor(b))) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Pyro Index",
    y = "log(Geometric Species Index)",
    color = "b (fixed)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p2)

```

```{r}
# --- Standalone Debugging Snippet ---

# 1. Define the parameters for the test case
b_test <- 7
# This is the theoretical 'k' where the turning point occurs for b=7
k_turning_point <- 0.6894

# 2. Define the Pyro Index function (copied from your thesis)
pyro_index <- function(b, k) {
  return(1 / k * (1 - log(b)) + lgamma(1 / k + 1))
}

# 3. Calculate the Pyro Index at the theoretical turning point
pyro_value_at_minimum <- pyro_index(b_test, k_turning_point)

# 4. Print the result
print(paste("For b =", b_test, "the theoretical minimum should be at Pyro Index =", round(pyro_value_at_minimum, 3)))

```



```{r}
# --- Pyro index formula ---
pyro_index <- function(b, k) {
  1/k * (1 - log(b)) + lgamma(1/k + 1)
}

# Parameters to sweep
b_vals <- seq(0.2, 3, length.out = 100)   # avoid b=0 or 1 for log(b)
k_fixed <- c(0.6, 0.7, 1, 1.5, 2)

# Data for fixed k
df_glm <- expand.grid(b = b_vals, k = k_fixed)

# Calculate pyro index and biodiversity index (GLM only)
df_glm$pyro <- mapply(pyro_index, df_glm$b, df_glm$k)
df_glm$logBio <- mapply(bio_index_thm, df_glm$b, df_glm$k)

# Pyro vs b
p1 <- ggplot(df_glm, aes(x = b, y = pyro, color = factor(k))) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  labs(x = "b", y = "Pyro Index", color = "k") +
  theme_minimal()

# log(Bio) vs b
p2 <- ggplot(df_glm, aes(x = b, y = logBio, color = factor(k))) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  labs(x = "b", y = "log(Geometric Mean Bio)", color = "k") +
  theme_minimal()

# log(Bio) vs Pyro
p3 <- ggplot(df_glm, aes(x = pyro, y = logBio, color = factor(k))) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  labs(x = "Pyro Index", y = "log(Geometric Mean Bio)", color = "k") +
  theme_minimal()

# Show plots
print(p1)
print(p2)
print(p3)


```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# Time range for plotting
t_plot <- seq(0.1, 100, length.out = 200)  

# Make a tidy data frame of all curves
df_power <- map_dfr(seq_along(alpha_list), function(i) {
  tibble(
    t = t_plot,
    species = species_list[i],
    value = alpha_list[i] * t_plot^beta_list[i]
  )
})

# Find y-axis limits (so all species share the same scale)
y_max <- max(df_power$value, na.rm = TRUE)

# Plot
ggplot(df_power, aes(x = t, y = value, color = species)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "t",
    y = "Power function value",
    title = "Species-specific Power Functions from GLM"
  ) +
  ylim(0, y_max) +
  theme_minimal()


```
